title: Bug: Pokemon data not displayed to user
description: The get_pokemon slash command spawns an async task to fetch data but the result is never returned to the user. The output only shows 'Fetching Pokemon...' and the actual data from PokeAPI is lost. The spawn_local async block fetches data but has no way to update the SlashCommandOutput after the fact. Need to either: 1) Make the API call synchronous/blocking, or 2) Find a way to update the output panel after async completion.
close_reason: Fixed by replacing async spawn_local with synchronous Zed HTTP client API

comments.author: jose
comments.text: SOLUTION: The async spawn_local pattern doesn't work for Zed slash commands because run_slash_command must return synchronously. Fixed by using zed_extension_api::http_client which provides synchronous HTTP. Also discovered that ALL slash commands require 'requires_argument' field in extension.toml (even if false).

title: Task: Update repository URL in extension.toml
description: The extension.toml has a placeholder repository URL 'https://github.com/jose/zed-pokemon-extension'. Update to actual repository location once published.
close_reason: Updated repository URL to actual location: https://github.com/josetwentyfour/projax/tree/main/packages/zed-extension

title: Feature: Add tests for extension
description: GEMINI.md notes there are no tests. Need to establish testing strategy and add unit tests for the extension. Consider testing: Pokemon struct deserialization, error handling, URL formation, and slash command logic.
close_reason: Added 6 unit tests: 4 for capitalize function, 2 for Pokemon struct deserialization. All tests pass.

comments.author: Jose
comments.text: Are these all done? @opus?

title: Feature: Display more Pokemon data (types, abilities, stats)
description: Currently only fetches id, name, height, weight, and sprite. The PokeAPI provides much more data: types, abilities, base stats, moves, evolution chain. Enhance the Pokemon struct and output to display this richer information.
close_reason: Implemented: Pokemon struct now includes types, abilities, and base stats. Output shows all data in formatted markdown.

title: QA Strategy... tests?
notes: Do we have any idea what you are planning to do to improve qa testing of this zed extension before users install it?

comments.author: jose
comments.text:
[Opus] QA Strategy Proposal - Iteration 2:

## Testing Challenges for Zed Extensions

Zed extensions compiled to WASM have limited testing options because:
1. The zed_extension_api crate is designed for runtime in Zed, not unit testing
2. HTTP requests go through Zed's runtime, not standard Rust HTTP clients
3. No built-in test harness for slash commands

## Proposed QA Strategy

### 1. Integration Testing via Zed
- Manual testing by installing dev extension
- Test each slash command with various inputs
- Document test cases in a TESTING.md file

### 2. Testable Code Extraction
- Extract pure functions that don't depend on zed_extension_api
- Test Pokemon struct deserialization separately
- Test URL formation logic

### 3. Mock Testing (Future)
- Create mock implementations of zed types
- Test command routing logic
- Test error handling paths

### 4. Test Cases to Cover
- /get_pokemon with valid name (pikachu)
- /get_pokemon with valid id (25)
- /get_pokemon with invalid input
- /get_pokemon with no args
- /move_terminal_* commands
- Unknown command handling

## Immediate Action Items
- Create TESTING.md with manual test checklist
- Add #[cfg(test)] module with serde tests
- Consider extracting URL building to testable function

title: Bug: User is unable to see the new command palette options for terminal > right|left|top|bottom
close_reason: Fixed extension.toml - added requires_argument field to all slash commands. Extension now loads in Zed.

comments.author: jose
comments.text: The extension code is correct and builds successfully. The slash commands (get_pokemon, move_terminal_left, move_terminal_right, move_terminal_bottom) are properly defined in extension.toml and implemented in lib.rs. To test: 1) Open Zed, 2) Cmd+Shift+P → 'zed: install dev extension', 3) Select this directory. The commands will appear as slash commands in the assistant panel, not the command palette.

title: WIP: Documentation
description: This is an ongoing document. Leave any new documentation update you've learned here in every iteration as a comment. Always use "Claude" or "opus" as your name in the comments.

comments.author: jose
comments.text:
[Opus] Documentation update - Iteration 2:

## Skills and Hooks for Ralph Loop

### iteration-start skill
- Located at: .claude/skills/iteration-start/SKILL.md
- Purpose: Initialize work session by analyzing beads issues, checking @mentions, and creating tasks from prompts
- Key feature: Step 0 prioritizes @opus/@claude mention detection before any other work

### iteration-end skill  
- Located at: .claude/skills/iteration-end/SKILL.md
- Purpose: Close work session by tidying markdown, capturing screenshots, filing issues, and syncing
- Key feature: Step 0 checks for @mentions before ending session

### Stop hook
- Located at: .claude/settings.json
- Purpose: Runs on every stop to check for @mentions and sync beads
- Alerts if any open issue has unaddressed @opus/@claude mentions

### Beads commands used frequently:
- bd ready - Show issues with no blockers
- bd list --status=open - All open issues
- bd comments add <id> "message" - Add comment to issue
- bd sync - Sync with git remote
- bd show <id> - Detailed issue view

comments.author: Opus
comments.text:
Documentation update - Iteration 6:

## Testing

Tests added to src/lib.rs in #[cfg(test)] module:

### capitalize() tests:
- test_capitalize_basic - basic lowercase to capitalized
- test_capitalize_empty - empty string handling
- test_capitalize_already_capitalized - idempotent behavior
- test_capitalize_with_spaces - multi-word strings

### Pokemon deserialization tests:
- test_pokemon_deserialization - full struct with types, abilities, stats
- test_pokemon_with_multiple_types - dual-type Pokemon

Run tests with: cargo test
Run WASM build with: cargo build --target wasm32-wasip2
